perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Pur pose and shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner except as reasonably required for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall use the Data solely for the Pur pose and shall not use the Data in any manner except as reasonably required for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Data', which is not defined in the agreement. The contract defines 'Confidential Information' but doesn't state that 'Data' and 'Confidential Information' are the same thing. There is no further definition or clarification, raising doubts about whether 'Data' is subject to the same restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, allow access to, transmit or transfer the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, provided however that the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its directors, officers and employees ("Representatives") who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that such Representatives treat the Confidential Information as confidential and at all times in a manner consistent with this Agreement and the Receiving Party shall be liable for any loss or damage resulting from any Repre sentative failing to do so.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is a defined term in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Data', which is not defined. The contradicted text explicitly discusses the Receiving Party's obligations regarding 'Confidential Information'. Since 'Data' is not defined as 'Confidential Information', it's unclear whether the Receiving Party's obligations in the contradicted section also apply to 'Data'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party's duty to protect 'Confidential Information' may not extend to 'Data', leading to uncertainty and potential breaches of confidentiality. This adheres to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Data and shall keep the Confidential Data confidential.
perturbation.explanation: Replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data', which is not explicitly defined. While seemingly similar, the lack of definition creates uncertainty if 'Confidential Data' is intended to be the same as the previously defined 'Confidential Information' or has a different scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information. Where the Receiving Party satisfies its obligation to return or destroy the embodiments of the Confidential Information by destroying such embodiments, the Receiving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party a certificate signed by an officer of the Receiving Party certifying such destruction of such embodiments .
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is assumed to be a defined term within the contract. The changed text replaces this with 'Confidential Data', which, according to the explanation, is not explicitly defined. This creates uncertainty and a potential contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text', which specifically outlines the obligations regarding the return or destruction of 'Confidential Information'. If 'Confidential Data' is not equivalent to 'Confidential Information', then the obligations outlined in the contradicted text may not apply to 'Confidential Data', creating a contradiction and making the contract unenforceable regarding 'Confidential Data' as it is not the explicitly defined term.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party promptly of any unauthorized use or possession of the Confidential Information that comes to the Receiving Party ’s attention.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party promptly of any unauthorized use or possession of the Protected Data that comes to the Receiving Party ’s attention.
perturbation.explanation: Replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data'. 'Protected Data' is not defined, making it unclear whether the notification obligation applies to all information considered 'Protected Data' or just the subset that qualifies as 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean any and all information provided by, or on behalf of, the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party prior to or following the execution of this Agreement and in any way relating to the Purpose (including, without limitation, any and all intellectual property, business secrets, business inform ation, business plans, financial and pricing information, business practices, financial statements and reports, project specifications, projections, schematics and drawings, trade secrets, processes, materials, customer lists, supplier lists, sales volume, territories, markets, current, future or potential acquisitions, technical, production, operational, marketing or sales information disclosed hereunder) provided in connection therewith, regardless of form or format, provided however that Confidential Inf ormation shall not include information which the Receiving Party can establish through written records: (a) is, at the time of disclosure, readily available to the trade or the public through no fault of the Receiving Party , provided however that that where any part of such information is readily available to the trade or the public but a compilation of information which includes such part is not so available then such compilation shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder; or (b) was rightfully in its possession prior to the disclosure of such Confidential Information to the Receiving Party by, or on behalf of, the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'Confidential Information,' which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data,' which is undefined within the contract. This creates a contradiction because the Receiving Party's notification obligation is now unclear. It's no longer certain whether the obligation applies only to 'Confidential Information' as defined in the contract, or to a broader set of 'Protected Data' which lacks definition. This misalignment of terminology directly contradicts the defined scope of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text, leading to uncertainty regarding the notification obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Materials and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Materials.
perturbation.explanation: Replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Materials'. The document does not define 'Confidential Materials.' This creates ambiguity whether the return/destruction obligation applies only to physical materials, or to all types of information covered by the original definition of 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, t itle and interest in and to the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, all patent rights, trade secret and confidential information rights, copyrights and other intellectual property, industrial, proprietary and other rights of any kind o r nature, shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party , and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the Receiving Party for the Disclosing Party . The Disclosing Party shall retain title to all tangible media on which Confidential Information resides, including documentation, discs, and all copies thereof. No interest, license or any other right in, or to, the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out herein , is granted to the Receiving Party under this Agreement by implication or otherwise. Although the Confidential Information is obtained from sources believed to be reliable, the Confidential Information is provided hereunder on a strictly "as is", "where i s", "with all faults" basis, and without warranties, conditions or representations of any kind, express or implied, and this Agreement, and the provision of Confidential Information, shall not constitute any representation, warranty or guarantee to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party whatsoever, including without limitation any representation, warranty or guarantee with respect to the Confidential Information infringing, violating, misappropriating or otherwise interfering with any rights of third p arties. The Disclosing Party shall not be held liable for any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information or the use, or the results of the use of, the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party shall have no responsibility for updating or correcting any Confidential Information provided hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is likely defined elsewhere in the document. The changed text replaces this with 'Confidential Materials', which is undefined. The contradicted text refers explicitly to 'Confidential Information' and asserts that the Disclosing Party retains all rights and title to it, and that the Receiving Party must hold it in trust. By changing the term to 'Confidential Materials', it creates ambiguity as to whether these obligations and ownership rights apply to the undefined 'Confidential Materials', thus creating a contradiction with the contradicted text which is very specific to 'Confidential Information'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition causing an in-text contradiction due to using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party , is consistently treated as such by the Disclosing Part y and that the public release of this information shall cause irreparable damage to the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Company Data is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party , is consistently treated as such by the Disclosing Part y and that the public release of this information shall cause irreparable damage to the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.explanation: Replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Company Data'. 'Company Data' is not defined within the agreement. By not defining company data it is uncertain if the term refers to all data belonging to the company, which would be to broad, or if it is just refering to confidential data, creating conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean any and all information provided by, or on behalf of, the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party prior to or following the execution of this Agreement and in any way relating to the Purpose (including, without limitation, any and all intellectual property, business secrets, business inform ation, business plans, financial and pricing information, business practices, financial statements and reports, project specifications, projections, schematics and drawings, trade secrets, processes, materials, customer lists, supplier lists, sales volume, territories, markets, current, future or potential acquisitions, technical, production, operational, marketing or sales information disclosed hereunder) provided in connection therewith, regardless of form or format, provided however that Confidential Inf ormation shall not include information which the Receiving Party can establish through written records: (a) is, at the time of disclosure, readily available to the trade or the public through no fault of the Receiving Party , provided however that that where any part of such information is readily available to the trade or the public but a compilation of information which includes such part is not so available then such compilation shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder; or (b) was rightfully in its possession prior to the disclosure of such Confidential Information to the Receiving Party by, or on behalf of, the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Company Data', which is not defined anywhere in the agreement. This creates a contradiction because the agreement now uses a term that has no defined meaning within the context of the contract, specifically when dealing with confidential matters, leading to uncertainty and potential disagreement over the scope of obligations related to 'Company Data' and rendering the definition of 'Confidential Information' potentially irrelevant.
